risk_note: Silver labels 62.5% questionable; single run temp=0; single structured prompt design tested.
recommended_revision: Add conditional: 'If gold supports silver labels (§VII) this pattern holds; otherwise downgraded per §VII.G criterion 3.' Add forward-ref to Table G3 GPT decision-tree and few-shot prompts as pre-registered additional prompt tests.

paper_claim: R4 complements LLM rather than replaces it
supporting_metric: r4_strong_f1_same_samples=0.3000 vs llm_strong_f1=0.0769; r4_macro_f1=0.3280 vs gpt_macro_f1=0.5523
risk_note: R4 macro-F1 0.22 lower than LLM; 98 supported→strong + 24 mild→strong false positives; FP/TP ratio not reported.
recommended_revision: Add explicit FP/TP ratio from existing data. Add: 'The complementarity claim holds if gold confirms that R4 strong recall remains higher than LLM strong recall (§VII H5).'

supporting_metric: strong_positive_delta=+0.1559 CI=[0.1058 0.1988] 10/10 seeds positive; strong_recall=0.4562 vs baseline 0.2062
risk_note: Screening use case not demonstrated operationally; FP cost not quantified; gold validation H6 is pre-registered but not yet run.
recommended_revision: Soften to 'R4 improves strong_action recall on silver labels; the screening use case is pre-registered for gold validation (Table G3 H6) and not yet demonstrated.' Add FP/TP ratio from existing R4 routing results.

paper_claim: Overall macro-F1 is not the target
supporting_metric: selection_criteria includes flat4_macro_not_down_gt_0.02 (R4 passes); R4 macro=0.4238 vs baseline 0.3963 (R4 improves macro on 444); on matched 100 R4 macro=0.3280 vs GPT 0.5523
risk_note: Reviewers may reject the 'macro-F1 is not the target' framing as excuse for poor macro-F1 on matched samples. The 0.3280 is substantially lower than LLM.
recommended_revision: Add: 'R4 is optimized under 5 pre-registered constraints (§IV.D); macro-F1 on the matched 100 is lower because R4 sacrifices supported/mild accuracy for strong recall. This trade-off is intentional and pre-registered.'

paper_claim: Evidence sufficiency calibration differs from support/refute
supporting_metric: NLI-only strong_f1=0.3934 vs action-gap-only=0.3648 vs combined=0.4209 (different signals); R4 routing order reflects theoretical principle
risk_note: The four-class taxonomy is a proposal; the ablation supports that different signals exist but doesn't prove the four classes are the right granularity.
recommended_revision: Add: 'The four-class taxonomy is a hypothesis; §VII pre-registers the test of whether the mild_vs_strong boundary is reliably drawable (κ≥0.40).'

paper_claim: Current result is pre-gold not final
section: Title page + VI opener + VII + IX Limitation 7 + X closer
risk_note: The 'pre-gold' framing is stated 14+ times; becomes repetitive and reads as apologetic (see F1).
recommended_revision: Reduce 'pre-gold' mentions to 4 key locations (Abstract §VI opener §VII opener §X closer). Reframe title page as 'silver-stage pilot with pre-registered gold validation protocol' instead of 'pre-gold complete draft.'

paper_claim: Gold validation is planned and frozen before annotation
supporting_metric: All TO_BE_FILLED_AFTER_GOLD; 6 success criteria + 6 downgrade criteria pre-registered
risk_note: The pre-registration is genuine and well-documented. Risk: a reviewer may see it as 'we wrote the protocol after seeing the silver results' (post-hoc).
recommended_revision: Add a date stamp in §VII: 'Protocol frozen 2026-07-04 in gold_pilot_protocol_freeze_v1/ before any gold annotation began.' The freeze report already documents this.

paper_claim: R4 improves strong_action positive-F1 from 0.2408 to 0.3967
risk_note: Computed against silver labels; 75% confusion rate on strong_action in author audit.
recommended_revision: Add: 'On silver labels' after every mention of 0.3967 in §VI.A. The conditional 'If gold supports silver labels' should appear at least once in §VI.A.

paper_claim: R4 is selected against 5 router variants under 5 pre-specified constraints
supporting_metric: R4 is the only router meeting all 5 constraints; R5 has higher strong_f1=0.4399 but fails flat4_macro_delta; R3 has 0.4047 but fails flat4_macro_delta
risk_note: The 5 constraints and 5 alternatives could be seen as rule-stacking (over-engineered selection).
recommended_revision: Add a table in §IV.D or appendix showing all 6 routers × 5 constraints with pass/fail. This transparency pre-empts the rule-stacking attack.

paper_claim: The structured 5-step prompt does not improve strong_action detection
risk_note: Single structured prompt design tested; other designs (decision-tree few-shot CoT tool-augmented) untested.
recommended_revision: Add forward-ref: 'Two additional prompt designs (decision-tree few-shot) are pre-registered for gold-subset evaluation (Table G3). If either closes the gap the claim is downgraded per §VII.G criterion 3.'

paper_claim: Author sanity audit flags 62.5% of silver labels as questionable/unclear
risk_note: This is the single biggest risk to the paper. The paper reports it honestly but doesn't fully grapple with the implication that all §VI numbers are computed against labels the authors themselves find questionable.
recommended_revision: Add in §VI opener: 'All results in this section are computed against silver labels that the author sanity audit (§V.D) flags as 62.5% questionable/unclear. The reader should interpret these numbers as pre-gold diagnostic evidence not as established performance.'

paper_claim: External SciFact/VitaminC results validate that R4-compatible methods run on public data
supporting_metric: SciFact NLI=0.6895 R4-compatible=0.3549; VitaminC NLI=0.6686 R4-compatible=0.4568
risk_note: R4-compatible underperforms NLI on public data; public data cannot test the four-class taxonomy; the 'validation' is a crash test not a performance test.
recommended_revision: Reframe as 'transferability probe' not 'validation'. Consider moving to appendix.

paper_claim: The current silver-stage evidence motivates gold validation rather than replacing it
risk_note: This is the right framing. Risk: it's stated 3 times (§VII.H §IX §X) and may read as defensive.
recommended_revision: Keep as the closing sentence in §X only. Remove from §VII.H and §IX (replace with specific downgrade criteria references).
